meaning: adj.类似的,相似的,可比拟的
examples: Leibniz developed closely analogous concepts and techniques.
The solar system also has two analogous classes of objects, smaller than planets—namely, asteroids and comets.
For example, a gap just below aluminum suggested a new element would be found with properties analogous to those of aluminum.
Videos and eye-catching infographics (analogous to the storyboards for a movie) are all over the place.
Are there analogous historical examples of the low versus high technology adoption in the world?
